metric_value: ~100 InSight briefs published Feb 2001 – Feb 2003
notes: Snapshot from Aberdeen free research portal, archived Feb 2003

metric_value: ~15 InSights on CRM topics spanning 2001-2003
notes: CRM was the single largest topic category in the catalog

metric_value: ~10 InSights on storage management and SAN/NAS
notes: Dan Tanner primary author for storage InSights

metric_value: ~12 InSights on WiFi, Bluetooth, 3G, semiconductors 2001-2003
notes: Russ Craig primary author for semiconductor/wireless InSights

metric_value: Linux will join IBM z/OS and Windows as primary server OSs before end of decade
notes: Bill Claybrook InSight July 2001; strong directional forecast

metric_value: Linux became dominant server OS by mid-2000s; now runs majority of cloud infrastructure
notes: Prediction proved largely correct within timeframe

metric_value: Shakeout in wireless LAN market started; many vendors will not remain in market a year from now
notes: Russ Craig InSight; predicted vendor consolidation

metric_value: WiFi market consolidated; Broadcom, Atheros, Intel emerged dominant; many early vendors exited
notes: Prediction proved correct; Broadcom/Atheros duopoly emerged

metric_value: 3G rollout delays signal global emerging trend, not just hype
notes: Kelly Quinn InSight; DoCoMo delays cited

metric_value: Global 3G rollout significantly delayed from 2001 projections; mass-market 3G emerged ~2007-2009
notes: Prediction of delays proved correct; 4G LTE not until 2010+

metric_name: Pocket PC vs Palm OS by 2005
metric_value: Pocket PC will eclipse Palm OS as leading handheld platform by 2005
notes: Ro/Wright/Fletcher InSight; bold prediction at time of Palm dominance

metric_name: Pocket PC vs Palm - Actual Outcome
metric_value: Neither won; smartphone revolution (iPhone 2007) rendered both obsolete; prediction was incorrect
notes: Prediction was directionally wrong; smartphones disrupted entire handheld category

metric_value: Nextel Boost subsidiary expected to help company be a key player in mobile youth market
notes: Dana Tardelli InSight

metric_value: Nextel merged with Sprint 2005; Boost Mobile became successful prepaid brand
notes: Prediction partially correct on Boost; Sprint-Nextel merger changed dynamics

metric_value: Exodus filed bankruptcy Sept 2001; C&W acquired assets for $850M
notes: Catalog documents the outcome directly

metric_value: Baan appears on sound financial footing after turnaround under Invensys
notes: Katherine Jones InSight; Baan was an Invensys company at time

metric_value: Baan sold by Invensys to SSA Global 2003; eventually acquired by Infor; viability prediction broadly correct short-term
notes: Company survived but changed hands multiple times

metric_value: Agile and Extreme Programming promote development reengineering rather than just control
notes: Wayne Kernochan InSight; early enterprise analyst validation of agile methods

metric_value: Growing number of IT buyers will divert resources from broad CRM suites to e-mail-centric customer lifecycle messaging
notes: Kent Allen InSight; prescient about marketing automation trend

metric_value: Enterprise IT planners should maintain open mind toward using IP for storage networking
notes: Dan Tanner InSight; IP storage (iSCSI) adoption proved correct

metric_value: Siebel 7.5 exposes business processes as Web services; methodology-driven CRM
notes: Denis Pombriant InSight

metric_value: Siebel acquired by Oracle 2006 for ~$5.85B after losing market share to SAP and Salesforce.com
notes: Siebel's best-of-breed vulnerability proved real

metric_value: Microsoft has significant .NET technical progress but still cannot answer IT's fundamental question: 'What is it?'
notes: Fletcher/Gardner InSight; .NET identity confusion was a real market problem

metric_value: .NET became well-established enterprise framework by 2004-2005; identity question resolved by mainstream adoption
notes: Assessment was accurate at time; .NET went on to wide adoption

metric_value: WorldCom filed bankruptcy July 2002; Michael Cappellas joined as CEO; emerged as MCI in 2004; acquired by Verizon 2006
notes: Catalogue notes Cappellas departure from HP to WorldCom just before HP's surprise profit announcement

metric_value: Post-Cappellas exit, HP announced top- and bottom-line growth surprising Wall Street; stock jumped 20%
notes: Kastner InSight; Aberdeen not caught off guard by HP results

metric_value: Standards are the only true path to the Holy Grail of an IT utility
notes: Dan Tanner InSight; prescient about storage standards role

metric_value: Worldwide BPM spending forecast and analysis (2001-2005) published; sector uses workflow-related solutions in grander domains
notes: Darcy Fowkes InSight

metric_value: Corporate spending on PLM technology will grow during flat economy

metric_value: SAP remains largest enterprise application vendor as of 2026; hegemony prediction proved correct

metric_value: Aberdeen field experience at odds with NY Times negative view of Computer Associates viability
